what_it_does: A posted note from Tim responding to a design point about replacing a continuous up/down scrub with stepped detents, haptic ticks, and live outline; he defers to the team's design judgment while reserving the ability to request changes later.
observations: Authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel
Type is note and state is posted
Tim says he does not understand all the design terms used
Tim accepts the team's proposed redesign away from free analog drag
Tim explicitly reserves the right to push back later using a referenced 'second mode'
standouts: Tim defers to design direction he admits he does not fully understand
Explicit reservation of future feedback/veto through a 'second mode'
Endorses stepped up/down interaction over smooth continuous scrub
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a [point] marker to frame the note
References another board item with a commented_on link
concerns: The 'second mode' system is mentioned but not explained in this file
The referenced original idea §2 and item-99a1d803 are not present here
notes: Tim's posted note deferring to the team's stepped-detent redesign of the drag interaction while keeping the right to redirect later via a feedback mode.
questions: What is the 'second mode' feedback system Tim refers to?
What is the content of the original idea §2 and item-99a1d803?
What are the concrete UX meanings of 'stepped detents', 'haptic ticks', and 'live outline' in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is labeled as a point/response note and contains exactly that — a design pushback and conditional assent.
intention_signals: To resolve a design disagreement by letting the team proceed with a refined stepped interaction, while preserving Tim's ability to request changes later.
novelty: Tim explicitly accepts a design direction he does not fully understand, contingent on a future feedback/override mechanism.
contribution: Records a stakeholder's consent to replace free analog drag with a stepped interaction and documents the expectation of a future feedback/override mechanism.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim deferring to the team's stepped-detent redesign of a drag interaction while reserving the right to request changes through a second feedback mode.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's design assent and conditional delegation for the drag interaction redesign.
